creator: dev@submerge.ch
text:
> Offhand, does anyone know of a set of circumstances where SCHED_ULE has clearly better
> performance than SCHED_4BSD?

Interactive programs, e.g. typical desktop applications. At least that's what I remember from the transition, but that was a long time ago.

> Upon reflection, why is idprio going to have any effect on dnetc at all under any
> circumstances?  dnetc is practically the paradigm of a compute-bound process that for
> all intents and purposes will NEVER be idle, so why should its idle priority matter?

As explained before, idle priority is about scheduling dnetc when it wants to run, not about it being idle. It works quite well in my experience. When I compile ports with idle priority I have almost no interference with my desktop applications - given there's no other bottlenecks like IO or RAM.

While the different buildworld times are an interesting finding, that's a batch job and may not be representative for other use cases and hardware setups. How is interactivity with SCHED_ULE and idprio dnetc?
